intent: Cut the first real tagged release, v0.2.1. No release has ever been tagged (only the floating v0 alias exists); package.json was at 0.2.0 but that release was never published, and the just-merged hardening PR (#37) fixed real security bugs (setup-protection clobber, provider key leak). Publishing v0.2.0 would ship the pre-hardening code, so v0.2.1 is the first honest release and carries the hardening. This change only performs the version-bump ceremony from RELEASING.md: bump package.json 0.2.0 -> 0.2.1, roll the CHANGELOG [Unreleased] section into [0.2.1] - 2026-07-08 with a fresh empty [Unreleased] above it, and update the compare links. No source, action, workflow, or policy changes. The v0.2.1 tag is pushed after this merges; the release workflow verifies tag==package.json version and publishes notes from the [0.2.1] section.

validation_plan.reason: Confirm the repo still type-checks after the version bump (defensive — no .ts changed, but the release workflow runs this against the tag so it must pass).
Full suite must stay green — the release workflow gates publication on it against the tagged commit.

validation_plan.command: npm run bundle && git diff --exit-code dist/index.js
validation_plan.reason: dist/index.js is unchanged (no source touched); the dist-check job confirms the committed bundle is byte-identical.
Version bump and CHANGELOG section must agree with the tag that will be pushed (v0.2.1); the release workflow fails if tag and package.json disagree.

execution_evidence.skip_reason: CI-covered by the test job (tsc runs in the build); no source changed.
CI-covered by the required test job.

execution_evidence.command: npm run bundle && git diff --exit-code dist/index.js

result_summary: package.json version 0.2.0 -> 0.2.1. CHANGELOG.md: the [Unreleased] section (all the #37 Security/Hardening notes) renamed to [0.2.1] - 2026-07-08, a fresh empty [Unreleased] added above it, and compare links updated ([Unreleased] now v0.2.1...HEAD, new [0.2.1] v0.2.0...v0.2.1). No src/, action.yml, workflow, or .plumbline policy changes — self_modifying:false. Verified version/CHANGELOG agreement locally; type-check, test, and dist-check are CI-covered and run again by the release workflow against the tagged commit before publishing.
